label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look at the question and the passage. The question is asking which of the following is NOT a reason the English language spread to numerous parts of the world. Looking at the first paragraph of the passage: "The English language was first introduced to the Americas by British colonization, beginning in 1607 in Jamestown, Virginia. Similarly, the language spread to numerous other parts of the world as a result of British trade and colonization elsewhere and the spread of the former British Empire, which, by 1921, held sway over a population of 470-570 million people, approximately a quarter of the world's population at that time." So the reasons given for the spread of English are British colonization, British trade, and the spread of the former British Empire. Now, looking at the answer choices: (A) British colonization - this is mentioned in the passage as a reason, so it is a valid reason. (B) the spread of the former English Empire - this is mentioned in the passage as a reason, so it is a valid reason. (C) British trade around the world - this is mentioned in the passage as a reason, so it is a valid reason. (D) a large British population - the passage mentions that the British Empire held sway over a large population (470-570 million people), but it doesn't state that a large British population itself was a reason for the spread of English.
